name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 08.12.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 08.12.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Sowing Status: As on December 05, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 479.02 lakh ha (including 79.88 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 77.84 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 451.12 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rabi Sowing Status: As on December 05, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 479.02 lakh ha (including 79.88 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard & 77.84 lakh ha in Gram) as compared to 451.12 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As  on  05.12.2025,  90.16%  of  area  has  been  harvested  for  Kharif  crops  all  over  India. Approximately,  61%  area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Uradbean  (100%),  Moongbean  (100%)  & Mothbean (100%); 94.46% area of Oilseeds majorly in Groundnut (94.83%),  Soyabean (100%)& Sesamum (92.28%); 94.36% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (95.28%) & Bajra (98.31%) and 78% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.
texts.text:  As  on  05.12.2025,  90.16%  of  area  has  been  harvested  for  Kharif  crops  all  over  India. Approximately,  61%  area  of  Pulses  mainly  in  Uradbean  (100%),  Moongbean  (100%)  & Mothbean (100%); 94.46% area of Oilseeds majorly in Groundnut (94.83%),  Soyabean (100%)& Sesamum (92.28%); 94.36% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals, majorly in Jowar (95.28%) & Bajra (98.31%) and 78% area of Rice has been harvested for crops of Kharif 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Minimum temperatures are likely to be below normal in most of India except in J&K, some parts of South Karnataka and some parts of NMMT region.
texts.text: Temperature: Minimum temperatures are likely to be below normal in most of India except in J&K, some parts of South Karnataka and some parts of NMMT region.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01-  December  07  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  22%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average (LPA), with regional variations:  (i) East & Northeast India: 6%; (ii) Northwest India: 68%; (iii) Central India: 41%; and South Peninsula: 11%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01-  December  07  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  22%  higher  than  its  Long  Period  Average (LPA), with regional variations:  (i) East & Northeast India: 6%; (ii) Northwest India: 68%; (iii) Central India: 41%; and South Peninsula: 11%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Western  Disturbance  now  seen  as  an  upper  air  cyclonic  circulation  over  north Pakistan and neighbourhood at 3.1 km above mean sea level with a trough aloft in middle tropospheric westerlies  with its axis at  5.8 km above mean sea level  now runs roughly along Long. 68°E to the north of Lat. 28 °N.
texts.text:  The  Western  Disturbance  now  seen  as  an  upper  air  cyclonic  circulation  over  north Pakistan and neighbourhood at 3.1 km above mean sea level with a trough aloft in middle tropospheric westerlies  with its axis at  5.8 km above mean sea level  now runs roughly along Long. 68°E to the north of Lat. 28 °N.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Subtropical westerly Jet Stream with core winds of the order upto 115 knots at 12.6 km above mean sea level continues to prevail over North India.
texts.text:  Subtropical westerly Jet Stream with core winds of the order upto 115 knots at 12.6 km above mean sea level continues to prevail over North India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Isolated to Scattered rainfall activity over Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-BaltistanMuzaffarabad, South Kerala, South Tamil Nadu and Islands.
texts.text:  Isolated to Scattered rainfall activity over Jammu-Kashmir-Ladakh-Gilgit-BaltistanMuzaffarabad, South Kerala, South Tamil Nadu and Islands.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Light to moderate rainfall very likely at a few places accompanied with Thunderstorm, lightning with Gusty Wind likely to prevail over Andaman & Nicobar Islands during 08 th -10 th  December.
texts.text:  Light to moderate rainfall very likely at a few places accompanied with Thunderstorm, lightning with Gusty Wind likely to prevail over Andaman & Nicobar Islands during 08 th -10 th  December.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Cold wave conditions prevailed at a few places over Chhattisgarh; at isolated places over Madhya Pradesh, north Vidarbha and Odisha during 8 th  - 9 th  December.
texts.text:  Cold wave conditions prevailed at a few places over Chhattisgarh; at isolated places over Madhya Pradesh, north Vidarbha and Odisha during 8 th  - 9 th  December.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Dense  fog  conditions  very  likely  to  prevail  during  early  morning  hours  in  isolated pockets of Assam & Meghalaya, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram & Tripura, West Madhya Pradesh Himachal Pradesh and over Odisha during many days of the week
texts.text:  Dense  fog  conditions  very  likely  to  prevail  during  early  morning  hours  in  isolated pockets of Assam & Meghalaya, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram & Tripura, West Madhya Pradesh Himachal Pradesh and over Odisha during many days of the week

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  04  December  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  158.624  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  86.4%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is  nearly 107.27 percent of last  year's storage  and 122.43 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  04  December  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  158.624  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  86.4%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (183.565  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is  nearly 107.27 percent of last  year's storage  and 122.43 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text:  The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.
texts.text:  Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against  a  requirement  of  164.05  lakh  quintals, ensuring  a  surplus of  14.60  lakh  quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against  a  requirement  of  164.05  lakh  quintals, ensuring  a  surplus of  14.60  lakh  quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (27 Nov - 03 Dec 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 21%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 03 Dec 2025) is higher than LPA by 23%.
texts.text:  During the week (27 Nov - 03 Dec 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 21%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 03 Dec 2025) is higher than LPA by 23%.
